name: OmopPatientPersonView
title: Patient → person (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: produce one flat row per Patient, preserving raw FHIR values. Stage 2 SQL does ConceptMap lookups (gender / race / ethnicity), resolves location FK, and assigns surrogate person_id.

Uses US Core extensions for race / ethnicity / birthsex. Birthsex preferred over Patient.gender for gender_concept_id (closer to OMOP semantics for biological sex).

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR Patient.id (Synthea UUID). → person_source_value + surrogate person_id.

column.name: gender
column.description: male/female/other/unknown. Fallback when birthsex absent. → gender_source_value, optional gender_concept_id.

column.name: us_core_birthsex
column.description: Biological sex at birth (M/F). Preferred for gender_concept_id over Patient.gender.

column.name: race_omb_code
column.description: OMB race ombCategory code (2106-3/2054-5/…/UNK). → race_concept_id via 'race' ConceptMap.

column.name: ethnicity_omb_code
column.description: OMB ethnicity ombCategory code (2135-2 Hispanic / 2186-5 Not Hispanic). → ethnicity_concept_id.

column.name: general_practitioner_ref
column.description: Reference string ('Practitioner/<uuid>' or 'urn:uuid:<uuid>'). → provider_id via tailing segment lookup.

column.name: address_city
column.description: → cdm.location.city. Used in composite key when matching existing location.

column.name: address_zip
column.description: → cdm.location.zip. 5-digit US ZIP (FHIR side preserves leading zero, unlike ETL-Synthea's R fread).

column.name: address_country
column.description: → unused in OMOP location (no country column in v5.3).
